text_excerpt:
EXAMINATION:  CT HEAD W/O CONTRAST Q111 CT HEAD

INDICATION:  ___ with PMH of SVT, CAD, TIA, HTN, HLD, anxiety who presents
with recurrent SVT causing falls with headache// please evaluate for trauma to
head s/p fall

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images of the brain were obtained without
contrast. Coronal and sagittal reformations as well as bone algorithm
reconstructions were provided and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP: 750 mGy - cm

COMPARISON:  CT head from ___.

FINDINGS: 

There is no evidence of acute infarction,hemorrhage,edema, or mass. There is
tiny chronic right cerebellar infarct, new since prior.  Chronic infarct in
the left internal capsule, left caudate head, putamen is unchanged from prior
exam.  There is prominence of the ventricles and sulci suggestive of
involutional changes.  Periventricular and subcortical hypodensities are
nonspecific, however likely due to chronic small vessel ischemic disease.

There is no evidence of fracture.  Small mucosal retention cysts in the left
maxillary sinus is seen.  The visualized portion of the remaining paranasal
sinuses, mastoid air cells, and middle ear cavities are clear.  Patient is
status post bilateral lens replacements.  Calcifications are seen at the
bilateral carotid siphons.  Soft tissue density in the bilateral external ear
canals are nonspecific, though likely cerumen.

IMPRESSION:


1. No acute intracranial abnormalities.
2. Small chronic infarct.

EXAMINATION:  HIP (UNILAT 2 VIEW) W/PELVIS (1 VIEW) RIGHT

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with fall, complaining of right hip pain with
standing.// fracture?       fracture?

TECHNIQUE:  3 frontal views of pelvis and proximal right femur

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

There is a nondisplaced comminuted fracture in the right superior pubic ramus.
The patient is s/p right total hip prosthesis, with non-cemented femoral stem
in overall anatomic alignment.  The femoral head component is symmetrically
seated within the acetabular component. No periprosthetic lucency to suggest
loosening and no osteolysis is detected.  No heterotopic ossification is seen.
No evidence of dislocation.  There are vascular calcifications.  There are
minimal degenerative change is in the left hip.

IMPRESSION: 

nondisplaced comminuted fracture in the right superior pubic ramus

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed by Dr. ___ with ___
on the telephone on ___ at 10:22 am, 7 minutes after discovery of the
findings.

EXAMINATION:  CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION:  History: ___ with SVT// evaluate for pulmonary edema

TECHNIQUE:  Upright AP view of the chest

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph ___

FINDINGS: 

Lung volumes are low compared to the previous study.  Mild to moderate
cardiomegaly persists.  The aorta is markedly tortuous and diffusely
calcified.  Mediastinal and hilar contours appear similar.  Crowding of
bronchovascular structures is present with mild pulmonary vascular congestion,
but no frank pulmonary edema.  Streaky atelectasis is noted in the lung bases.
No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax is seen. 
Dextroscoliosis of the thoracic spine is re-demonstrated.

IMPRESSION: 

Low lung volumes with mild pulmonary vascular congestion but no frank
pulmonary edema.  Mild bibasilar atelectasis.
